evals.question_short: Add extension_foo.log_query bool GUC — _PG_init, hooks?, read-from-C, MarkGUCPrefixReserved

assertions.text: Names DefineCustomBoolVariable as the right entry point
Shows it called inside _PG_init
States that valueAddr (bool *) is the address of a static/extern backing variable; reads from C come from that variable directly, no getter needed
States that for a plain boolean toggle no check/assign/show hooks are needed — pass NULL/NULL/NULL
Calls MarkGUCPrefixReserved AFTER all DefineCustom* calls (ordering matters)
Explains MarkGUCPrefixReserved does TWO things: (a) remove existing placeholders, (b) refuse future ones (typo-catching)
Notes that parallel-worker startup would later trip over uncleaned placeholders (operational impact)
Notes EmitWarningsOnPlaceholders is the old name, MarkGUCPrefixReserved the new one (or that both exist)
Picks a sensible GucContext (PGC_SUSET or PGC_USERSET or PGC_SIGHUP, with reasoning)
Cites at least one file:line in source/ (guc.h, guc.c, worker_spi, or README)

evals.question_short: foo.cache_dir string GUC with directory-existence check_hook trio

assertions.text: Uses DefineCustomStringVariable with char ** valueAddr
check_hook signature: bool check(char **newval, void **extra, GucSource source)
check_hook returns false on rejection (not true on rejection)
Uses GUC_check_errdetail / GUC_check_errhint / GUC_check_errmsg / GUC_check_errcode for error reporting — NOT ereport(ERROR) directly
States that the assign_hook cannot fail (no return value), so fallible work belongs in check_hook
Notes that the check_hook may run OUTSIDE a transaction (bootstrap / postmaster startup / config reload), so catalog lookups must be guarded with IsTransactionState()
States that string-GUC storage replacement must use guc_malloc / guc_strdup, NEVER palloc, and the old value must be guc_free()'d
Notes that the check_hook may also be called purely to validate (not assign) — must be side-effect-free
assign_hook may run during transaction rollback → no catalog lookups
show_hook signature returns const char * and may use a static buffer (not reentrant)
Cites a source file (guc.h, guc.c, README, or worker_spi.c) with a line range

evals.question_short: PGC_USERSET trap for postmaster-bound worker count; GUC_LIST_INPUT vs GUC_LIST_QUOTE for identifier list

assertions.text: Rejects PGC_USERSET for foo.worker_count because the count is bound at postmaster startup
Recommends PGC_POSTMASTER as the right context (or PGC_SIGHUP with caveat)
Notes that the rule of thumb is 'loosest context that's still safe' — picking PGC_POSTMASTER because reconfiguring would require restarting workers / re-requesting shared memory
Names GUC_LIST_INPUT as the flag that enables comma-separated list parsing

States that GUC_LIST_INPUT alone is INSUFFICIENT for identifier lists — without GUC_LIST_QUOTE, an identifier that needs quoting (embedded space or uppercase) won't round-trip through pg_dump / pg_dumpall
Names GUC_LIST_QUOTE as the flag that double-quotes each element on serialization (matching search_path behaviour)
Suggests using SplitIdentifierString (or SplitGUCList) in the check_hook to parse the list
Notes that any string replacement in check_hook must use guc_malloc / guc_strdup, not palloc
Cites guc.h line range for flag bit values (~214-242) or a similar source anchor

iter1_vs_iter2_delta.baseline: +1 (18 → 19; baseline noise; eval-1 'AFTER all DefineCustom* calls' landed this run; eval-3 'GUC_LIST_INPUT alone insufficient' landed this run; iter-1 had 'Notes that the check_hook may also be called purely to validate' that did not land in iter-2 baseline)
